_**Agent (copilot/claude-sonnet-4.5)**_

I'll analyze the booking calendar and research how similar systems handle guest arrivals and departures. Let me examine the current implementation first.

After reviewing the code and calendar implementation, I can see the current system shows:
- Daily occupancy (X/45 people)
- All bookings for each day
- A modal with booking details when clicking "+X more"

## Analysis of Similar Systems

Here are common approaches used by booking/hotel management systems:

### 1. **Visual Indicators on Bookings**
- **Check-in marker**: Green dot/arrow/icon on arrival day
- **Check-out marker**: Red dot/arrow/icon on departure day
- **Staying indicator**: Different background for mid-stay days

### 2. **Dedicated Arrival/Departure Views**
- **Timeline view**: Shows bookings as horizontal bars
- **Split day view**: Each day divided into "Arrivals | Staying | Departures"

### 3. **Enhanced Calendar Cell**
- **Separate sections** within each day cell:
  - Top: Arrivals count (↓ 5 arriving)
  - Middle: Current guests
  - Bottom: Departures count (↑ 3 leaving)

## Recommended Solution for Your Cottage

Given your 13 rooms and 45-person capacity, I recommend a **hybrid approach**:

### Option A: Enhanced Calendar with Arrival/Departure Badges

_**Agent (copilot/claude-sonnet-4.5)**_

Looking at the booking list view, I suggest adding **inline status dropdown menus** that allow quick status changes without needing to navigate to the detail page. Here's my recommended solution:

## Recommended Approach: Inline Status Dropdowns

Add dropdown menus directly in the table cells for both booking status and invoice status. This provides:

✅ **Quick updates** - Change status without leaving the page
✅ **Visual feedback** - Color-coded badges show current status
✅ **Minimal clicks** - Single click to open, single click to select
✅ **No page reload** - Uses LiveView events for instant updates

## Implementation

````elixir
// filepath: /Users/michalhorcic/projects/ELIXIR/cozy_checkout/lib/cozy_checkout_web/live/booking_live/index.html.heex
<%!-- In the table body, replace the status badge cells with dropdowns --%>

<%!-- Booking Status Column --%>
<.table.td>
  <div class="relative" id={"booking-status-#{booking.id}"}>
    <button
      phx-click={JS.toggle(to: "#status-menu-#{booking.id}")}
      class="w-full text-left"
    >
      <%= case booking.status do %>
        <% "upcoming" -> %>
          <span class="px-2 inline-flex text-xs leading-5 font-semibold rounded-full bg-blue-100 text-blue-800">
            Upcoming
          </span>
        <% "active" -> %>
          <span class="px-2 inline-flex text-xs leading-5 font-semibold rounded-full bg-green-100 text-green-800">
            Active
          </span>
        <% "completed" -> %>
          <span class="px-2 inline-flex text-xs leading-5 font-semibold rounded-full bg-gray-100 text-gray-800">
            Completed
          </span>
        <% "cancelled" -> %>
          <span class="px-2 inline-flex text-xs leading-5 font-semibold rounded-full bg-red-100 text-red-800">
            Cancelled
          </span>
      <% end %>
    </button>
    
    <div
      id={"status-menu-#{booking.id}"}
      class="hidden absolute z-10 mt-1 w-48 rounded-md shadow-lg bg-white ring-1 ring-black ring-opacity-5"
    >
      <div class="py-1" role="menu">
        <button
          phx-click="update_booking_status"
          phx-value-id={booking.id}
          phx-value-status="upcoming"
          class="block w-full text-left px-4 py-2 text-sm text-gray-700 hover:bg-gray-100"
        >
          <span class="px-2 inline-flex text-xs leading-5 font-semibold rounded-full bg-blue-100 text-blue-800">
            Upcoming
          </span>
        </button>
        <button
          phx-click="update_booking_status"
          phx-value-id={booking.id}
          phx-value-status="active"
          class="block w-full text-left px-4 py-2 text-sm text-gray-700 hover:bg-gray-100"
        >
          <span class="px-2 inline-flex text-xs leading-5 font-semibold rounded-full bg-green-100 text-green-800">
            Active
          </span>
        </button>
        <button
          phx-click="update_booking_status"
          phx-value-id={booking.id}
          phx-value-status="completed"
          class="block w-full text-left px-4 py-2 text-sm text-gray-700 hover:bg-gray-100"
        >
          <span class="px-2 inline-flex text-xs leading-5 font-semibold rounded-full bg-gray-100 text-gray-800">
            Completed
          </span>
        </button>
        <button
          phx-click="update_booking_status"
          phx-value-id={booking.id}
          phx-value-status="cancelled"
          class="block w-full text-left px-4 py-2 text-sm text-gray-700 hover:bg-gray-100"
        >
          <span class="px-2 inline-flex text-xs leading-5 font-semibold rounded-full bg-red-100 text-red-800">
            Cancelled
          </span>
        </button>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</.table.td>

<%!-- Invoice Status Column --%>
<.table.td>
  <%= if booking.invoice do %>
    <div class="relative" id={"invoice-status-#{booking.id}"}>
      <button
        phx-click={JS.toggle(to: "#invoice-menu-#{booking.id}")}
        class="w-full text-left"
      >
        <%= case booking.invoice.state do %>
          <% "created" -> %>
            <span class="px-2 inline-flex text-xs leading-5 font-semibold rounded-full bg-gray-100 text-gray-800">
              Created
            </span>
          <% "personal" -> %>
            <span class="px-2 inline-flex text-xs leading-5 font-semibold rounded-full bg-purple-100 text-purple-800">
              Personal
            </span>
          <% "generated" -> %>
            <span class="px-2 inline-flex text-xs leading-5 font-semibold rounded-full bg-blue-100 text-blue-800">
              Generated
            </span>
          <% "sent" -> %>
            <span class="px-2 inline-flex text-xs leading-5 font-semibold rounded-full bg-yellow-100 text-yellow-800">
              Sent
            </span>
          <% "paid" -> %>
            <span class="px-2 inline-flex text-xs leading-5 font-semibold rounded-full bg-green-100 text-green-800">
              Paid
            </span>
        <% end %>
      </button>
      
      <div
        id={"invoice-menu-#{booking.id}"}
        class="hidden absolute z-10 mt-1 w-48 rounded-md shadow-lg bg-white ring-1 ring-black ring-opacity-5"
      >
        <div class="py-1" role="menu">
          <button
            phx-click="update_invoice_status"
            phx-value-id={booking.invoice.id}
            phx-value-state="created"
            class="block w-full text-left px-4 py-2 text-sm text-gray-700 hover:bg-gray-100"
          >
            <span class="px-2 inline-flex text-xs leading-5 font-semibold rounded-full bg-gray-100 text-gray-800">
              Created
            </span>
          </button>
          <button
            phx-click="update_invoice_status"
            phx-value-id={booking.invoice.id}
            phx-value-state="personal"
            class="block w-full text-left px-4 py-2 text-sm text-gray-700 hover:bg-gray-100"
          >
            <span class="px-2 inline-flex text-xs leading-5 font-semibold rounded-full bg-purple-100 text-purple-800">
              Personal
            </span>
          </button>
          <button
            phx-click="update_invoice_status"
            phx-value-id={booking.invoice.id}
            phx-value-state="generated"
            class="block w-full text-left px-4 py-2 text-sm text-gray-700 hover:bg-gray-100"
          >
            <span class="px-2 inline-flex text-xs leading-5 font-semibold rounded-full bg-blue-100 text-blue-800">
              Generated
            </span>
          </button>
          <button
            phx-click="update_invoice_status"
            phx-value-id={booking.invoice.id}
            phx-value-state="sent"
            class="block w-full text-left px-4 py-2 text-sm text-gray-700 hover:bg-gray-100"
          >
            <span class="px-2 inline-flex text-xs leading-5 font-semibold rounded-full bg-yellow-100 text-yellow-800">
              Sent
            </span>
          </button>
          <button
            phx-click="update_invoice_status"
            phx-value-id={booking.invoice.id}
            phx-value-state="paid"
            class="block w-full text-left px-4 py-2 text-sm text-gray-700 hover:bg-gray-100"
          >
            <span class="px-2 inline-flex text-xs leading-5 font-semibold rounded-full bg-green-100 text-green-800">
              Paid
            </span>
          </button>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  <% else %>
    <span class="text-gray-400">—</span>
  <% end %>
</.table.td>
````

````elixir
// filepath: /Users/michalhorcic/projects/ELIXIR/cozy_checkout/lib/cozy_checkout_web/live/booking_live/index.ex
// ...existing code...

@impl true
def handle_event("update_booking_status", %{"id" => id, "status" => status}, socket) do
  booking = Bookings.get_booking!(id)
  
  case Bookings.update_booking(booking, %{status: status}) do
    {:ok, _booking} ->
      {:noreply,
       socket
       |> put_flash(:info, "Booking status updated successfully")
       |> load_bookings()}
    
    {:error, _changeset} ->
      {:noreply, put_flash(socket, :error, "Failed to update booking status")}
  end
end

@impl true
def handle_event("update_invoice_status", %{"id" => id, "state" => state}, socket) do
  invoice = Bookings.get_booking_invoice!(id)
  
  case Bookings.update_booking_invoice_state(invoice, state) do
    {:ok, _invoice} ->
      {:noreply,
       socket
       |> put_flash(:info, "Invoice status updated successfully")
       |> load_bookings()}
    
    {:error, _changeset} ->
      {:noreply, put_flash(socket, :error, "Failed to update invoice status")}
  end
end

// ...existing code...
````

## Alternative: Modal Approach

If you prefer a more confirmatory approach with additional context:

- Click status badge → Opens a modal
- Modal shows current status + available transitions
- Can add validation messages (e.g., "Can't mark as paid without invoice number")
- More space for helpful text

## My Recommendation

Start with the **inline dropdown** approach because:
1. It's faster for bulk operations
2. Less visual interruption
3. Status changes are usually straightforward
4. You can always add confirmation dialogs later for critical transitions

Would you like me to implement the inline dropdown solution, or would you prefer the modal approach?
